data.message: At Guest Hall (Return to the Gathering, R1), with 1 real action remaining and 2 resources, the action menu offered only 'Play Mind over Matter' (a Fast event) and 'Pass'. Missing options that should be legal: Move to Bedroom or Bathroom (both listed as connections in ./ahlcg state), Take resource (always a legal basic action), Play Flashlight (cost 2, affordable), Play Scrying (cost 1, affordable). Guest Hall's only textual restriction is 'cannot take draw actions' (confirmed via ./ahlcg card Guest Hall), which explains the missing Draw option but not the others. Investigate to Guest Hall (0 clues there) is also missing, which may be correct if the engine omits investigates at 0-clue locations, but per the rules you can still investigate a 0-clue location to trigger 'after successful investigate' reactions (e.g. Dr. Milan Christopher's resource gain) even though 0 clues would be discovered.
No enemy to fight/evade and no other legal action offered; ending turn 1 with 1 clue banked toward Act1's 3-clue requirement

data.message: Reproducible pattern across R1 and R2: whenever exactly 1 real (non-Tome-bonus) action remains in the Investigation phase, the action menu incorrectly drops all normal 1-action options (Move, Take resource, Play [cost] cards) and only offers Fast events (e.g. Mind over Matter, which costs 0 actions) plus Pass. R1: after Move to Guest Hall left '1 action left', menu omitted Move to Bedroom/Bathroom, Take resource, Play Flashlight (2 res, affordable), Play Scrying (1 res, affordable) - confirmed via a probe invalid-option call that these truly weren't legal choices at that decision point. R2: same thing at Bedroom after Investigate left '1 action left' - Move to Guest Hall, Take resource, Play Flashlight/Scrying/Working a Hunch all missing. In both cases the SAME actions were freely available one action-count earlier (2 actions left) and should still cost only 1 action to perform with 1 remaining. This looks like the last normal action is being conflated with Daisy's separate Tome-only bonus action slot (which legitimately restricts to Tome abilities only) - i.e. an off-by-one in how 'actions left' is computed/gated for the action menu. This is costing me one real action nearly every round.
No fight/evade needed and no other action offered by the engine at this decision point; ending turn
